title: Compile source-backed A-roll edit decisions

adversarial_denial: Given a proposed cut through a word or an unsupported paraphrase represented as source speech, when validation runs, then the EDL is rejected before rendering.
required_evidence: Word-boundary validation, source/output time map, EDL hash, phrase references, and cut-purpose receipt.

required_evidence: Migration manifest, artifact classification, test isolation, and claim-ceiling validation.

title: Emit immutable source-grounded Visual Asset Demands through Delegation
normative_requirement: When a source-backed content job lacks or must repair a visual ingredient, the Pipeline shall emit an immutable Visual Asset Demand through Delegation containing the exact source lineage, semantic and composition role, asset family, geometry need, permitted variation, preservation locks, budget, deadline, and evaluation contract.
trigger_preconditions: A Workflow Node proves that an accepted composition or edit requires an unavailable, incompatible, or failed visual ingredient.
required_output_state: A versioned Delegation request linked to the source package, Expression Moment or evidence object, Harness, node, and composition/timeline program.
authority_invariants: Pipeline does not plan VAE production strategy. | The demand cannot mutate source meaning. | Source-derived identity/expression references remain attributable.
positive_acceptance: Given a Carousel slide requiring a source-grounded portrait cutout, when the asset is unavailable, then the demand includes the exact keyframe/source-time reference and intended composition region.
adversarial_denial: Given an agent that decides to generate a decorative image without an accepted role or demand, when admission runs, then Delegation is not invoked.
required_evidence: Demand hash, lineage graph, authority check, budget reservation, and Delegation submission receipt.

title: Consume VAE Asset Results with image-conditioned geometry
normative_requirement: The Pipeline shall consume accepted VAE Asset Results only after validating request/result identity, source and derivative lineage, asset-family scope, measured geometry, masks, gaze or motion, depth/layers, validated crops, negative-space regions, limitations, evaluation evidence, and downstream compatibility.
trigger_preconditions: Delegation returns a candidate or accepted Asset Result.
required_output_state: A validated production-asset reference and geometry pack eligible for the requesting Composition or VideoEditProgram.
authority_invariants: An asset result cannot silently change the demand. | Limitations remain visible downstream. | The producing model cannot self-approve.
positive_acceptance: Given a returned portrait derivative, when validation passes, then the Pipeline can place it using measured face/subject BBOXes and validated crop data without rerunning the producer.
adversarial_denial: Given a visually attractive asset with missing demand identity or evaluator evidence, when continuation is requested, then the Pipeline blocks the downstream node.
required_evidence: Demand/result match report, geometry validation, evaluator receipts, artifact hashes, and acknowledgement/usage receipt.

title: Keep SAM3 execution inside the Visual Asset Editor
normative_requirement: SAM3 segmentation and tracking shall be implemented, versioned, evaluated, and operated by the Visual Asset Editor behind its provider and capability interfaces; the Pipeline owns only demand, result, health, and receipt integration.
trigger_preconditions: A VAE route requires concept-conditioned segmentation or tracking.
required_output_state: VAE-owned masks, tracks, geometry evidence, model/runtime identities, and Asset Result references.
authority_invariants: No SAM3 worker is implemented inside Pipeline core. | Pipeline does not claim VAE provider capability. | Provider output remains independently evaluated.
positive_acceptance: Given a VAE plan selecting SAM3, when the worker succeeds, then the Pipeline receives only the contract-defined result and evidence.
adversarial_denial: Given a Pipeline node attempting to call SAM3 directly to bypass VAE planning, when ownership validation runs, then the action is denied.
required_evidence: Product-boundary test, VAE capability receipt, Delegation result, and Pipeline consumer conformance.

title: Keep Lucida matting evaluation inside the Visual Asset Editor
normative_requirement: Lucida or any replacement matting provider may be evaluated and, if authorized, executed only within the VAE capability registry and production plan; the Pipeline shall not own provider selection or matting semantics.
trigger_preconditions: The VAE determines that alpha refinement is required for an asset family and route.
required_output_state: VAE-owned matte artifacts, benchmark/evaluation evidence, limitations, and Asset Result bindings.
authority_invariants: License and model evidence must be approved. | Provider choice remains VAE-owned. | Pipeline sees only typed result status and artifacts.
positive_acceptance: Given an approved Lucida capability and a typography cutout demand, when VAE executes, then matte evidence is packaged with the result.
adversarial_denial: Given an unreviewed model or license, when a production plan tries to bind it, then VAE blocks before execution and Pipeline receives a typed blocker.
required_evidence: Capability registry state, benchmark report, dependency adoption review, output hashes, and result receipt.

title: Keep layered visual generation and GNM behind VAE capability routing
normative_requirement: Layered image providers, ComfyUI workflows, and Google GNM facial-geometry references shall remain VAE-owned capabilities selected by the Visual Production Plan; they are not Pipeline, Interview Expression, or identity-authority components.
trigger_preconditions: A Visual Asset Demand requires layer-separated generation, controlled facial geometry, expression reference, pose/gaze reference, or another specialist visual route.
required_output_state: VAE plan decisions, bounded provider artifacts, geometry/reference passes, evaluation, and Asset Result lineage.
authority_invariants: GNM does not infer human identity or emotional truth. | Real-person identity comes from approved source references and fitting evidence, not demographic sampling. | Provider outputs cannot approve themselves.
positive_acceptance: Given an approved source-backed Expression Moment and demand for a controlled head-pose reference, when VAE selects GNM, then the resulting mesh/render references preserve source identity lineage and are labeled geometric controls.
adversarial_denial: Given a request to generate a real coach identity from GNM demographic labels, when validation runs, then the route is prohibited.
required_evidence: VAE route receipt, source-reference binding, GNM/version identity, geometry outputs, fairness/limitation acknowledgement, and independent evaluation.

title: Validate Asset Results and downstream usage before continuation
normative_requirement: The Pipeline shall resume composition only after the VAE result passes demand identity, authority, source lineage, geometry, evaluation, artifact integrity, supersession, and usage-acknowledgement checks.
trigger_preconditions: A VAE result is proposed for use by a static, temporal, or animation derivative.
required_output_state: An accepted production asset binding, downstream usage record, invalidation dependencies, or typed rejection.
authority_invariants: Result-ready is not completed until downstream acknowledgement where required. | Rejected assets remain historical. | Supersession invalidates only affected descendants.
positive_acceptance: Given an evaluated result with complete geometry, when the requesting program acknowledges exact use, then the Pipeline resumes and records the consumption relationship.
adversarial_denial: Given a superseded or revoked asset, when a cached composition attempts to use it, then eligibility fails and only affected descendants are invalidated.
required_evidence: Validation report, usage acknowledgement, asset/version lineage, and selective invalidation receipt.

title: Verify mechanically decidable conditions
normative_requirement: Contracts, geometry, text fit, timing, source lineage, tool use, export and execution-envelope conditions shall use deterministic verification.

required_output_state: A versioned, inspectable result or governed record that satisfies this requirement and can be consumed by the declared downstream owner.
authority_invariants: Producer cannot approve itself. | Failure attribution distinguishes retrieval/model/tool/runtime/evaluator. | Repair changes only permitted units.
positive_acceptance: Given current authority, valid inputs, and an eligible execution binding, when this behavior runs, then contracts, geometry, text fit, timing, source lineage, tool use, export and execution-envelope conditions shall use deterministic verification and the downstream consumer receives a contract-valid result.
adversarial_denial: Given stale, ineligible, ambiguous, out-of-scope, or unverifiable inputs, when this behavior is requested, then no unauthorized side effect occurs and a typed blocker names the cause, owner, and next admissible action.
required_evidence: deterministic validators, evaluator calibration, rendered reparse, diagnosis accuracy, selective rerun, and escalation, linked to an immutable requirement receipt.

title: Persist complete production and human-resolution episodes
normative_requirement: Each accepted, rejected, repaired, escalated, and human-resolved production episode shall link the source package, Harness, run state, JIT Capsule, candidates, exact tool operations, renders, evaluations, decision authority, result, and downstream outcome.
trigger_preconditions: A node, candidate portfolio, operator action, or campaign reaches a decision or terminal state.
required_output_state: Immutable ProductionEpisode and, where human authority acted, HumanResolutionEpisode records.
authority_invariants: UI actions cannot disappear as anonymous state. | Human scope and rationale remain explicit. | No episode mutates live models or canonical doctrine.
positive_acceptance: Given an operator moves a cut and accepts the preview, when the action commits, then the episode records before/after program state, exact tool command, affected nodes, evaluation, and acceptance scope.
adversarial_denial: Given a manual change with no attributable authority or before-state identity, when persistence is requested, then the change cannot become accepted state.
required_evidence: Episode hash, before/after references, tool/process receipts, human authority, and result lineage.

title: Attribute learning opportunities to the responsible layer
normative_requirement: The system shall diagnose whether an episode indicates a source, knowledge, retrieval, context, Skill, Steering Recipe, Programmed Model, tool, runtime, VAE, evaluator, or human-policy opportunity before it enters any learning queue.
trigger_preconditions: A production or human-resolution episode is closed.
required_output_state: A typed attribution record, candidate programming-material dispositions, and excluded interpretations.
authority_invariants: Visible symptoms are not automatically assigned to the model. | Attribution can remain uncertain or multi-causal. | Promotion requires later evidence.
positive_acceptance: Given a bad crop caused by a wrong geometry measurement, when diagnosis runs, then the episode is attributed to measurement/runtime rather than used to teach a taste model.
adversarial_denial: Given a single operator rejection with no reason, when attribution is ambiguous, then the system stores it as scoped preference evidence but does not promote a universal recipe.
required_evidence: Diagnosis report, responsible-layer confidence, excluded alternatives, and human review where required.

title: Compile operator-authorized versioned programming-material corpora
normative_requirement: Supervised demonstrations, preference pairs, hard negatives, repair trajectories, retrieval-obedience cases, and revision-program examples shall be built offline from eligible episodes with exact source authority, operator source authorization, provenance, scope, splits, hashes, and invalidation.
trigger_preconditions: A model or evaluator team requests a dataset build for a bounded capability claim.
required_output_state: Versioned corpus manifest, training/validation/test partitions, exclusions, source authority/operator source authorization scope, and source episode references.
authority_invariants: Real-human source authority govern downstream training. | Protected test data is isolated. | No live-production mutation occurs.
positive_acceptance: Given approved video-revision episodes, when a corpus is built, then source authority and identity scope are checked and the test split remains unavailable to the producer.
adversarial_denial: Given an episode whose operator source authorization does not permit model training, when dataset selection runs, then it is excluded while remaining available for allowed operational audit if permitted.
required_evidence: Dataset manifest, operator-declared source authority report, split hashes, deduplication, leakage tests, and build receipt.

title: Capture human preferences and corrections with explicit scope
normative_requirement: Every operator approval, rejection, candidate selection, natural-language revision, direct manipulation, tool override, taste explanation, and publication decision shall produce a HumanResolutionEpisode whose applicability scope is explicit and nonuniversal by default.
trigger_preconditions: A human takes an authorized action in any Studio surface or review queue.
required_output_state: A HumanResolutionEpisode with request, before/after state, operations, reasons, authority, learning scope, and candidate dispositions.
authority_invariants: One decision never silently becomes universal doctrine. | Direct UI manipulation and text requests produce equivalent evidence quality. | Human identity is never synthesized on replay.
positive_acceptance: Given a Carousel slide reorder, when the operator commits it, then the exact order change and stated reason are recorded with category/profile scope.
adversarial_denial: Given an operator drag event without a resolved project/run/authority identity, when commit is attempted, then the UI must request resolution rather than writing anonymous state.
required_evidence: Episode record, authority scope, exact changes, tool program, evaluation, and acceptance.

title: Promote Steering Recipes only through repeated evidence
normative_requirement: Repeated successful, scoped interventions may advance a Steering Recipe through proposed, experimental, validated, shadow, limited-production, production, deprecated, and retired states with control comparisons, regression cases, human authority, and rollback.
trigger_preconditions: Episode analysis identifies a recurring condition/intervention pattern.
required_output_state: A versioned Steering Recipe proposal or lifecycle transition with applicability, preserved properties, evidence, controls, failures, and rollback.
authority_invariants: A raw winning prompt is not a recipe. | A single success cannot change defaults. | Contradictory and failed evidence remains linked.
positive_acceptance: Given ten comparable hierarchy repairs and controls showing consistent improvement, when promotion is reviewed, then the recipe may enter shadow with exact scope and rollback.
adversarial_denial: Given one visually appealing result and no control or failure cases, when promotion is requested, then the recipe remains proposed.
required_evidence: Recipe evidence ledger, control comparison, regression suite, human promotion receipt, and rollback package.

title: Prohibit uncontrolled online mutation while enabling automatic evidence capture
normative_requirement: Production may automatically append immutable episodes, retrieval indexes, and programming-material eligibility records, but it shall not rewrite model weights, canonical Skills, Steering Recipes, authority rules, or production defaults without an offline evaluated release and human authorization.
trigger_preconditions: A production episode or human correction completes.
required_output_state: Automatic evidence/index updates and, separately, an offline promotion candidate if eligible.
authority_invariants: Capture is automatic; promotion is not. | Historical evidence remains immutable. | Release rollback is always possible.
positive_acceptance: Given an accepted correction, when the episode closes, then retrieval can find it immediately while the production model remains unchanged.
adversarial_denial: Given a service attempting to update an adapter checkpoint directly from a live click stream, when policy enforcement runs, then the mutation is blocked and incident evidence is recorded.
required_evidence: Mutation-denial tests, index update receipt, model artifact immutability checks, and promotion workflow.

title: Expose source-to-batch run and graph projections
normative_requirement: The Studio Control Tower shall show the Canonical Interview Source Package, content-batch jobs, Harnesses, phases, Workflow Nodes, bounded roles, owners, status, blockers, costs, artifacts, source-time lineage, and exact execution evidence.
trigger_preconditions: An operator opens a project, source package, campaign, or run.
required_output_state: Live read models for source, batch, run graph, artifacts, evidence, human gates, and next admissible actions.
authority_invariants: Read models do not become authority stores. | Synthetic/dry-run/real artifact classes are unmistakable. | Role labels do not hide actual execution actors.

adversarial_denial: Given a UI fixture or stale projection that cannot prove its source state, when displayed, then it is labeled nonauthoritative and cannot drive a command.
required_evidence: Read-model consistency tests, realtime event mapping, source-lineage drill-down, and projection freshness receipts.

title: Provide category-native supervisory previews over canonical programs
normative_requirement: Static, video, interview, VAE, and knowledge/model Studio modules shall display the current canonical programs, source evidence, assets, candidates, evaluations, repairs, and allowed human actions without owning hidden production state.
trigger_preconditions: A run reaches a reviewable state or an operator enters a category-native Studio.
required_output_state: Interactive projections of Composition IR, VideoEditProgram, source transcript/time map, VAE demands/results, and model/retrieval evidence.
authority_invariants: UI state round-trips into typed commands. | Reopening reconstructs from canonical records. | Direct manipulation cannot bypass validation.
positive_acceptance: Given a video cut moved by drag, when committed, then the Studio emits the same typed ChangeRequestProgram as an equivalent natural-language request.
adversarial_denial: Given a timeline edit stored only in browser memory, when the page reloads, then it is not accepted and the canonical timeline remains unchanged.
required_evidence: Round-trip tests, command receipts, reload/replay tests, and UI/canonical-state consistency.

title: Apply Harness autonomy modes and explicit human gates
normative_requirement: Each campaign shall operate in AUTOPILOT, REVIEW_BEFORE_SHIP, CHECKPOINTED, or SHADOW mode, pausing only at declared human authority nodes, genuine exceptions, budget or policy changes, or unresolved taste boundaries.
trigger_preconditions: A campaign is launched or a node encounters a human-owned condition.
required_output_state: An autonomy-policy binding, human-gate records, exception package, and resume decision.
authority_invariants: Mature Harnesses target exception-only supervision. | Humans are not asked to approve deterministic facts. | No publication occurs beyond the mode’s authority.
positive_acceptance: Given REVIEW_BEFORE_SHIP mode, when all production nodes pass, then one final acceptance is requested without intermediate pauses unless a genuine exception occurred.
adversarial_denial: Given SHADOW mode, when all outputs pass evaluation, then publication remains prohibited.
required_evidence: Autonomy binding, pause/resume logs, authority tests, and publication-denial receipt.

title: Inspect retrieval, Skills, recipes, Programmed Models, and exclusions
normative_requirement: Operators shall be able to inspect the JIT Capsule, retrieved Skills and Steering Recipes, authority filters, exclusions, contradictory evidence, model claim envelope, benchmark evidence, limitations, fallbacks, and the reason each item influenced the current decision.
trigger_preconditions: A learned, retrieval-backed, or taste-bearing node is reviewed.
required_output_state: A human-readable evidence view linked to exact retrieval and model receipts.
authority_invariants: Similarity never appears as authority. | Protected evaluator internals remain hidden. | Unavailable context is disclosed.
positive_acceptance: Given a static strategy proposal, when inspected, then the operator sees the selected Skill, two eligible recipes, one relevant negative precedent, and the exact exclusions.
adversarial_denial: Given a semantically similar but wrong-category recipe, when the operator inspects retrieval, then it appears only in the exclusion evidence and not the active JIT Capsule.
required_evidence: Retrieval receipt, claim manifest, benchmark links, and UI evidence tests.

title: Expose runtime, provider, worker, and budget health
normative_requirement: The Studio shall expose preflight, compatibility, health, queue, lease, resource, cost, and failure evidence for Skia, PRETEXT, FFmpeg, Remotion, HyperFrames, VAE providers, GNM, workers, and other bound embodiments without treating their presence as production certification.
trigger_preconditions: An operator inspects a binding, campaign, active worker, blocker, or incident.
required_output_state: Capability and worker health projections, budget usage, exact blocker cause, owner, and next admissible action.
authority_invariants: Health does not imply quality certification. | Credentials and secrets are never exposed. | Unavailable capabilities cannot be silently emulated.
positive_acceptance: Given a failed Remotion worker preflight, when inspected, then the Studio shows the exact dependency/version failure and blocks only the affected nodes.
adversarial_denial: Given a fake provider result or synthetic URI, when health/result views load, then the artifact is clearly nonproduction and cannot satisfy completion.
required_evidence: Health receipts, worker heartbeats, budget ledger, blocker records, and security tests.

title: Export source-to-ship audit and replay packages
normative_requirement: A completed campaign shall export the source package manifest, content-batch plan, Harnesses and bindings, JIT Capsules, typed programs, HumanResolutionEpisodes, artifacts, evaluations, execution fingerprints, logs, publication evidence, and replay instructions needed to reconstruct the accepted result.
trigger_preconditions: A campaign reaches accepted, rejected, canceled, shadow-complete, or published terminal state.
required_output_state: A portable audit/replay bundle with exact hashes and claim boundaries.
authority_invariants: No secrets or absolute machine paths. | Historical human decisions are replayed as recorded rather than regenerated. | External missing dependencies are declared.
positive_acceptance: Given a published short and Carousel, when the audit package is opened elsewhere, then the source lineage and all accepted decisions can be verified even if rendering dependencies are unavailable.
adversarial_denial: Given an artifact with no source or execution fingerprint, when export runs, then the bundle fails completeness validation.
required_evidence: Portable bundle validation, hash manifest, secret/path scan, and clean-room replay report.

title: Build the source-first interview-to-content-batch reference slice
normative_requirement: Release 1 shall admit one real Activative Interview or imported interview, produce an accepted Canonical Interview Source Package, launch a source-backed batch, render one talking-head A-roll short and one Carousel or SuperVisual from the same source, execute bounded evaluation and repair, record at least one HumanResolutionEpisode, and preserve exact source lineage.
trigger_preconditions: Program Control authorizes the bounded Release 1 evidence campaign.
required_output_state: Real source package, content-batch plan, real short video, real static derivative, evaluation/repair receipts, human-resolution evidence, and audit bundle.
authority_invariants: No fake URIs or synthetic assets count as real output. | Talking-head A-roll remains the short’s base timeline. | Format 02 is excluded.
positive_acceptance: Given one accepted interview source, when the reference slice completes, then both outputs trace to exact source spans and pass technical, semantic, composition, and source-lineage gates.
adversarial_denial: Given only a static synthetic fixture or a generated script with no interview source, when release evidence is assessed, then Release 1 remains incomplete.
required_evidence: Real-file hashes, source-time maps, render/evaluation receipts, HumanResolutionEpisode, and operator acceptance.

title: Require complete source-first release evidence
normative_requirement: The Release 1 slice shall prove source authority and identity, dual-admission correctness, Harness and binding authority, retrieval relevance, program validity, renderer correctness, independent evaluation, local repair, human resolution capture, sandbox, cancellation/replay, fallback, and truthful claim ceilings.
trigger_preconditions: Release readiness is evaluated.
required_output_state: A release evidence matrix, blockers, scope claims, and readiness receipt.
authority_invariants: One happy path cannot certify the product. | Implementation completion is not evidence closure. | Human participant and source authority are explicit.
positive_acceptance: Given the reference slice and required negative cases, when the matrix passes, then only the bounded source-first scope may be promoted.
adversarial_denial: Given missing evaluator calibration or source authority evidence, when readiness is computed, then production readiness remains false.
required_evidence: Case-family reports, evaluator metrics, recovery/replay, source authority/operator source authorization evidence, and release receipt.

title: Reconcile current repository and operator-local truth before implementation
normative_requirement: Implementation shall not begin until public and operator-local Builder, VAE, Delegation, Studio predecessor, campaign-amendment, and source-crosswalk states are reconciled into one Program Control status without rewriting historical receipts.
trigger_preconditions: The PRD or an implementation campaign seeks authorization.
required_output_state: One current status receipt, exact source hashes, resolved conflicts, and a bounded implementation allowlist.
authority_invariants: Historical receipts remain immutable. | Unverified local paths are not silently authoritative. | All products consume the same current status.
positive_acceptance: Given newer local terminal receipts, when verified, then Program Control publishes a superseding current status linked to both old and new evidence.
adversarial_denial: Given operator-reported files that cannot be read or hashed, when reconciliation runs, then implementation remains blocked.
required_evidence: Status reconciliation report, source manifests, conflict dispositions, and Program Control receipt.

title: Expand one source, category, runtime, provider, or learned claim boundary at a time
normative_requirement: Every expansion beyond Release 1 shall declare its source-package requirements, category/profile, derivative contract, runtime or provider binding, learned claim envelope, evaluator profile, source authority scope, rollback, and scope-specific evidence.
trigger_preconditions: A team proposes a new profile, derivative, runtime, provider, model claim, or source type.
required_output_state: An expansion proposal, evidence cut, compatibility manifest, and bounded authorization decision.
authority_invariants: No automatic inheritance of certification. | Category identities remain distinct. | New providers do not become product authority.
positive_acceptance: Given a second short-video archetype, when proposed, then its source/sequence/evaluation differences and benchmark cases are explicit.
adversarial_denial: Given a successful Carousel recipe, when reused for a SuperVisual without category evidence, then expansion is denied.
required_evidence: Expansion manifest, benchmark/evaluation plan, compatibility tests, and authorization receipt.

title: Separate implementation, evidence closure, production readiness, and certification
normative_requirement: Feature code, local tests, successful real outputs, production readiness, evaluator certification, and product certification shall remain separate claim states with independent evidence and authority.
trigger_preconditions: Any status, UI, report, or release process describes completion.
required_output_state: A truthful claim-state projection and maximum supported claim.
authority_invariants: Offline implementation cannot close real evidence gates. | Capability presence cannot confer certification. | Synthetic and real artifacts are distinct.
positive_acceptance: Given a real render but no recovery evidence, when status is displayed, then it may claim a real bounded output but not production readiness.
adversarial_denial: Given all unit tests pass, when a release report tries to claim certified, then claim-ceiling enforcement rejects it.
required_evidence: Claim-state tests, evidence matrix, status receipts, and UI labeling checks.

title: Generate a bounded PRD-to-implementation handoff
normative_requirement: After human ratification, the PRD shall produce a canonical FR ledger, value-based Epics, vertical CBAR-hardened Stories, Tech Spec candidates, exact source reuse dispositions, target paths, tests, evidence gates, Development Capsules, and authority boundaries without treating the planning package as implementation authorization.
trigger_preconditions: The updated PRD passes structural and authority review.
required_output_state: Planning package, traceability matrices, candidate spec inventory, source allowlists, and next authorization request.
authority_invariants: Every FR has one primary Story owner. | Every Story names downstream failure and proof. | Specs read exact brownfield files before design.
positive_acceptance: Given the ratified PRD, when the planning factory runs, then all requirements map to one primary Story and candidate specs identify exact source paths and boundaries.
adversarial_denial: Given an unmapped requirement or duplicate primary owner, when validation runs, then the handoff fails.
required_evidence: Traceability validation, Story/FR ownership report, source-crosswalk validation, and planning receipt.

title: Establish the Canonical Interview Source Package as the batch root
normative_requirement: The product shall represent each interview-derived campaign with one immutable Canonical Interview Source Package that binds the accepted source media, transcript, speakers, timestamps, tags, Expression Moments, keyframes, source authority, and provenance used by every derivative job.
trigger_preconditions: An Activative Interview session completes or an existing interview is uploaded for production.
required_output_state: A stable source_package_id, version, manifest, source assets, transcript structures, evidence objects, source authority scope, and downstream lineage root.
authority_invariants: Every derivative references this root. | The package records facts and evidence but does not itself create a content strategy. | Original source bytes and historical versions remain immutable.
positive_acceptance: Given an accepted interview and transcript, when the package is finalized, then a short-video, Carousel, and SuperVisual job can independently resolve the exact source objects they use.
adversarial_denial: Given a derivative request with no accepted source_package_id, when batch admission runs, then the request is rejected as ungrounded.
required_evidence: Package schema validation, source/hash manifest, lineage graph, and acceptance receipt.

title: Support Activative Interview Brief-led admission
normative_requirement: The Interview Expression integration shall admit sessions produced from an Activative Interview Brief, preserving planned Context Premises, Matrix of Edging, anchors, Interview Asset Contracts, intended tags, operator source authorization, and session design separately from what the participant actually expressed.
trigger_preconditions: An approved Activative Interview Brief and recording session are supplied.
required_output_state: A source package containing the Brief lineage, planned activation fields, recording assets, observed expression evidence, and planned-versus-observed comparison.
authority_invariants: Planned intent is never represented as actual expression. | Human reaction remains source evidence. | Unsupported Brief expectations may remain unmet without fabrication.
positive_acceptance: Given a Brief seeking a specific tension, when the interview does not produce it, then the package records the planned tag as unconfirmed and does not create an Expression Moment.
adversarial_denial: Given a planned anchor with no matching source span, when downstream routing runs, then it cannot be used as quote or evidence.
required_evidence: Brief hash, session operator source authorization, planned/observed tag comparison, and Interview Expression acceptance receipt.

title: Support imported interview and transcript admission as a first-class path
normative_requirement: The product shall admit an existing interview video with an uploaded or generated transcript without pretending that an Activative Interview Brief, Matrix of Edging, planned anchors, or predeclared asset contracts existed.
trigger_preconditions: An operator uploads an interview or talking-head video and transcript, or requests transcription.
required_output_state: A source package with imported-source provenance, transcript/speaker alignment, retrospective evidence candidates, operator-confirmed context, and explicit absent-planning fields.
authority_invariants: Imported sessions receive equal downstream production capability after validation. | Missing upstream planning is disclosed, not synthesized. | Retrospective inferences remain labeled until confirmed.
positive_acceptance: Given a legacy interview with no Brief, when admitted, then the system can still produce a trusted source package and launch the same derivative Harnesses.
adversarial_denial: Given a model-inferred Matrix of Edging represented as if it had guided the original interview, when validation runs, then the claim is rejected.
required_evidence: Import receipt, source/transcript hashes, absent-field declaration, retrospective-tag evidence, and operator confirmation.

title: Align transcript, speakers, time bases, and media technically
normative_requirement: The source-package compiler shall reconcile word-level timestamps, phrase boundaries, speaker diarization, audio events, source frame/time bases, media duration, and transcript confidence before source spans become eligible.
trigger_preconditions: Source media and a transcript or transcription result are available.
required_output_state: Word and phrase records, speaker map, audio-event track, alignment confidence, source-time conversion functions, and blockers.
authority_invariants: No source span exceeds media bounds. | Speaker changes and phrase breaks are reproducible. | Low-confidence regions remain visible.
positive_acceptance: Given multi-speaker interview audio, when alignment completes, then every eligible phrase references one speaker and exact source times within tolerance.
adversarial_denial: Given transcript drift beyond the declared tolerance, when Expression Moment extraction starts, then the affected ranges are blocked or routed for correction.
required_evidence: Alignment benchmark, duration/time-base validation, diarization review samples, and phrase-pack hash.

title: Govern operator-declared source authority, operator-defined source handling, and derivative usage
normative_requirement: The source package shall declare participant operator source authorization, ownership, allowed derivative formats, publication scope, model-training eligibility, identity/voice use, retention, source revocation, and restricted evidence before any job or learning disposition is admitted.
trigger_preconditions: Source material enters the workspace or its permitted use changes.
required_output_state: A versioned source authority-and-operator source authorization envelope linked to source assets, derivatives, training eligibility, and source revocation/invalidation policy.
authority_invariants: No job exceeds the granted scope. | Training source authority are independent from production source authority. | Source revocation preserves audit history while preventing new prohibited use.
positive_acceptance: Given operator source authorization for social derivatives but not model training, when the content batch runs, then production may proceed while dataset compilation excludes the source.
adversarial_denial: Given expired or withdrawn publication authority, when a publish command runs, then it is denied and affected unpublished derivatives are blocked.
required_evidence: Operator source-authority declaration, provenance checks, scope tests, usage audit, and source-revocation simulation.

title: Version, supersede, and selectively invalidate source packages
normative_requirement: Corrections to transcripts, speakers, tags, source authority, Expression Moments, or source assets shall create a new immutable source-package version and compute selective downstream invalidation rather than rewriting accepted history.
trigger_preconditions: An operator or validator corrects an accepted source-package field or the operator source-authority scope changes.
required_output_state: A superseding package version, field-level change set, affected-job graph, invalidation proposals, and historical reproduction path.
authority_invariants: Original package versions remain reproducible. | Only dependent derivatives are invalidated. | Human decisions are never regenerated during replay.
positive_acceptance: Given a corrected speaker assignment in one phrase, when supersession occurs, then only derivatives using that phrase are invalidated.
adversarial_denial: Given a corrected transcript, when a service mutates the original package in place, then integrity validation fails.
required_evidence: Version/hash comparison, dependency analysis, selective invalidation tests, and historical replay.

title: Separate planned, observed, inferred, confirmed, rejected, and superseded tags
normative_requirement: Every source and routing tag shall declare its provenance state, evidence, confidence, authority, applicability, and lifecycle so an intended interview topic cannot be confused with an observed Expression Moment or an operator-confirmed route.
trigger_preconditions: A Brief, transcript analysis, source observation, operator decision, or supersession produces a tag.
required_output_state: Typed tag records and relationships to source spans, evidence, Brief items, archetypes, and downstream jobs.
authority_invariants: Tag states never collapse silently. | Inference cannot outrank confirmed evidence. | Rejected tags remain available as negative evidence.
positive_acceptance: Given a planned 'authority conflict' tag and an observed unrelated answer, when routing runs, then only observed/confirmed tags influence the job unless the operator resolves the gap.
adversarial_denial: Given an inferred tag with no supporting source span, when it is proposed for a quote card, then eligibility fails.
required_evidence: Tag-state registry tests, provenance graph, evidence links, and exclusion receipt.

title: Use the packed phrase-level transcript as the primary language reasoning surface
normative_requirement: The Pipeline shall compile a compact, speaker-aware, time-annotated phrase transcript from cached word-level data and use it as the default text reasoning surface, loading raw words or visual composites only when the current decision requires them.
trigger_preconditions: Transcript alignment is accepted.
required_output_state: A versioned phrase pack with source IDs, start/end times, speaker, words, audio events, tags, and links to source evidence.
authority_invariants: Raw word data remains canonical for timing. | Compression cannot remove editorially meaningful fillers or events without declaration. | Phrase packing is deterministic.
positive_acceptance: Given a one-hour interview, when a Moment Hunter runs, then it receives the compact phrase pack plus only the relevant raw words and visual evidence.
adversarial_denial: Given phrase packing that normalizes away a hesitation used as an expression signal, when validation compares it with raw ASR, then the pack is rejected.
required_evidence: Phrase-pack hash, compression coverage, source-word backreferences, and retrieval/context-size benchmark.

title: Build source shot maps, transitions, keyframes, and visual references
normative_requirement: The source-package compiler shall detect existing shot boundaries and transitions, sample representative and expression-relevant keyframes, record camera/framing changes, and expose visual references without treating shot changes as semantic importance.
trigger_preconditions: Video source media passes technical admission.
required_output_state: A shot map, transition labels, keyframe set, visual-quality notes, face/subject geometry where available, and source-time references.
authority_invariants: Shot detection does not create Expression Moments. | Keyframes remain linked to exact source frames. | Model uncertainty and missed-transition risk are visible.
positive_acceptance: Given an already edited interview, when shot analysis runs, then downstream edit planning can avoid cutting across source dissolves and can retrieve representative frames.
adversarial_denial: Given a detected shot boundary, when no meaningful expression or quote exists, then it is not automatically routed as content.
required_evidence: Detection benchmark, frame hashes, source-time validation, and sampled human review.

title: Discover and approve Anchor Hits and Expression Moments
normative_requirement: A bounded Expression Moment Hunter may propose source-backed spans using transcript, audio events, keyframes, Brief context, tags, and evidence; an Analyst validates truth and completeness, and only the authorized approval path may promote an Expression Moment.
trigger_preconditions: An accepted source package is ready for expression discovery.
required_output_state: Candidate Anchor Hits and Expression Moments with source spans, quotes, speakers, evidence, expression qualities, archetype opportunities, confidence, and verdicts.
authority_invariants: The Hunter cannot approve, route, or invent unsupported quotes. | Expression meaning remains source-backed. | Rejected candidates remain historical.
positive_acceptance: Given a charged answer with a complete premise and reaction tail, when proposed and validated, then it may become an approved Expression Moment with exact source lineage.
adversarial_denial: Given a catchy partial quote that reverses the speaker's meaning outside context, when the Analyst checks it, then promotion is denied.
required_evidence: Candidate portfolio, source-context review, Analyst report, approval receipt, and false-positive/false-negative benchmark.

title: Preserve rejected and borderline expression evidence
normative_requirement: Rejected, incomplete, invalid, low-confidence, or borderline Anchor Hit and Expression Moment candidates shall be stored with reasons, responsible failure layer, and future admissibility conditions rather than discarded.
trigger_preconditions: Expression candidate evaluation returns other than accepted.
required_output_state: A rejected/borderline evidence record linked to source spans, verdict dimensions, reviewer, and possible repair or re-evaluation condition.
authority_invariants: Rejected evidence cannot enter production routing. | Negative examples remain source authority-scoped. | A later superseding decision is additive.
positive_acceptance: Given a powerful quote with insufficient operator source authorization for publication, when evaluated, then it remains a restricted candidate and cannot enter a content job.
adversarial_denial: Given a rejected candidate, when retrieval searches for production-ready moments, then it is excluded but may appear as required negative evidence.
required_evidence: Lifecycle and retrieval tests, rejection reasons, source authority scope, and supersession history.

title: Compile an Expression Ingredient Inventory and Asset Package Spec
normative_requirement: Interview Expression shall compile approved quotes, evidence, Expression Moments, keyframes, voice/audio references, visual references, tags, archetype opportunities, restrictions, and lineage into an Asset Package Spec that downstream Atomic Harnesses can consume without rediscovering the source.
trigger_preconditions: Expression discovery and source validation reach an approved handoff state.
required_output_state: A versioned Expression Ingredient Inventory and Asset Package Spec linked to the Canonical Interview Source Package.
authority_invariants: The package exposes possibilities, not final derivative meaning. | Each ingredient carries source and authority. | Unavailable or restricted ingredients remain explicit.
positive_acceptance: Given an approved session, when the package is compiled, then a short-video and Carousel Harness can select ingredients with no loss of source-time or evidence lineage.
adversarial_denial: Given a quote without exact words or source time, when the package is validated, then it is rejected.
required_evidence: Schema validation, completeness report, ingredient/source graph, restrictions, and handoff receipt.

title: Compile one source-backed content-batch program
normative_requirement: The Pipeline shall compile a Content Batch Orchestration Program from one accepted source package or Asset Package Spec, declaring target derivative jobs, Harnesses, shared ingredients, dependencies, budgets, deadlines, review modes, and source-lineage obligations.
trigger_preconditions: An operator selects a source package, objectives, target outputs, and approved Harnesses.
required_output_state: A versioned batch program and job graph for short videos, Carousels, SuperVisuals, animation derivatives, or other authorized outputs.
authority_invariants: The batch does not change source meaning. | Each job remains independently cancelable and attributable. | Shared dependencies are explicit.
positive_acceptance: Given one interview and a request for two shorts plus one Carousel, when compiled, then the jobs share source analysis while preserving separate Harness and evaluation requirements.
adversarial_denial: Given an output type with no current Harness, when batch compilation runs, then that job is blocked without blocking unrelated eligible jobs.
required_evidence: Batch schema, dependency graph, budget plan, Harness binding validation, and compilation receipt.

title: Route approved expression material through eligible archetypes and Harnesses
normative_requirement: The Pipeline shall route approved expression material through current archetype coalitions, Primitive Coalition Contracts, asset-derivative routes, category/profile registries, and Builder-produced Harnesses; archetypes are psychological geometries that organize the viewer role inside tension, not decorative labels.
trigger_preconditions: An Asset Package Spec and target content objectives are available.
required_output_state: Ranked route proposals with source support, expected viewer function, required ingredients, exclusions, and selected Harness identity.
authority_invariants: Archetype route is not source truth. | Wrong-category and unsupported routes are excluded before ranking. | Human creative authority may resolve genuine alternatives.
positive_acceptance: Given an Expression Moment that supports a myth-debunk sequence, when routing runs, then it proposes the matching current short-video Harness and explains evidence.
adversarial_denial: Given a strong quote that lacks the evidence needed by a data-story route, when routing runs, then that route is excluded.
required_evidence: Eligibility tests, route ranking benchmark, exclusion reasons, and route-selection receipt.

title: Create immutable derivative job contracts
normative_requirement: Each batch job shall bind one Harness version, exact source-package and ingredient references, derivative type, target platform/profile, intended Activative and sequence function, output constraints, runtime capabilities, evaluation, source authority, and review policy.
trigger_preconditions: A batch route is selected.
required_output_state: An immutable ContentDerivativeJob contract and dependency links.
authority_invariants: Jobs cannot read arbitrary source material outside their grant. | Job intent and source references are separately versioned. | Execution binding cannot mutate the contract.
positive_acceptance: Given a source-led short job, when admitted, then it contains exact source spans or a bounded selection contract and the talking-head A-roll rule.
adversarial_denial: Given a job whose requested platform exceeds source authority scope, when admitted, then it is rejected before execution.
required_evidence: Job schema, authority/source authority filters, source-grant validation, and admission receipt.

title: Share source analysis while preventing duplicate or conflicting derivatives
normative_requirement: The batch runtime shall reuse accepted source transcripts, phrase packs, shot maps, keyframes, and approved moments, deduplicate overlapping derivative candidates, and detect conflicting source uses or near-duplicate outputs before production spend.
trigger_preconditions: Multiple jobs or candidate routes use the same source package.
required_output_state: Shared read-only source-analysis bindings, deduplication groups, conflict warnings, and accepted diversity plan.
authority_invariants: Shared analysis is immutable. | Deduplication does not erase distinct archetype purposes. | Conflicts are explicit.
positive_acceptance: Given two proposed shorts using more than the allowed source overlap for the same hook, when batch planning runs, then the system keeps the stronger or requires a differentiated route.
adversarial_denial: Given identical transcript analysis requested by three jobs, when scheduled, then it is reused rather than rerun.
required_evidence: Reuse-cache tests, overlap/dedupe report, diversity evidence, and cost savings receipt.

title: Represent Hunters, Analysts, Composers, and Commanders as bounded workflow roles
normative_requirement: The Pipeline shall model Hunter, Analyst, Composer, and Commander responsibilities as explicit node roles with separate capabilities, tools, inputs, outputs, authority, and failure handling rather than hidden personas or a monolithic multi-agent prompt.
trigger_preconditions: A Harness compiles a source-discovery, evaluation, composition, or gate responsibility.
required_output_state: Role-labeled Workflow Nodes and typed handoffs mapped to actual execution actors.
authority_invariants: Role is independent from model or service identity. | Hunters do not approve; Composers do not self-evaluate; Commanders authorize only assigned transitions. | Deterministic implementations may fill roles.
positive_acceptance: Given Expression Moment discovery, when the workflow compiles, then Hunter proposals flow to an Analyst and an authorized approval node before routing.
adversarial_denial: Given a single model configured to hunt, compose, approve, and evaluate its own output, when ownership validation runs, then the graph is rejected.
required_evidence: Role/actor taxonomy tests, handoff contracts, authority matrix, and node receipts.

title: Propagate source or batch changes through selective invalidation
normative_requirement: When a source package, approved moment, route, source authority state, Harness, or shared ingredient is superseded, the batch runtime shall compute and rerun only affected jobs and descendants while preserving accepted unaffected work.
trigger_preconditions: A shared upstream object changes or is revoked.
required_output_state: Dependency impact report, invalidation events, preserved checkpoints, rerun plan, and operator notification.
authority_invariants: No blanket regeneration without dependency evidence. | Revoked source authority block new use immediately. | Historical outputs remain auditable.
positive_acceptance: Given one Expression Moment quote correction, when superseded, then only derivatives that use it are invalidated.
adversarial_denial: Given an unrelated keyframe correction, when a short uses no such frame, then its accepted render remains valid.
required_evidence: Dependency-graph tests, invalidation receipts, checkpoint reuse, and historical audit.

title: Require talking-head A-roll as the base timeline for source-led shorts
normative_requirement: Every interview-derived source-led short-video Harness shall preserve the original talking-head footage and source audio as the base temporal spine; B-roll, graphics, evidence, animation, captions, and reframing may support or temporarily interrupt it only under declared functions.
trigger_preconditions: A source-backed short-video derivative job is admitted.
required_output_state: A VideoEditProgram whose primary A-roll track is composed from exact source spans and whose supporting tracks declare their relation to A-roll.
authority_invariants: Generated visuals cannot replace the source expression as if they were the speaker. | Source voice remains attributable. | Any full-frame interruption preserves return and context.
positive_acceptance: Given a 45-second interview short, when the program is validated, then the A-roll track covers the approved spoken spine and all non-A-roll intervals have declared support functions.
adversarial_denial: Given a fully generated video using a paraphrased script but labeled as an interview-derived short, when validation runs, then the job fails the A-roll requirement.
required_evidence: A-roll coverage report, source-span map, support-layer manifest, and semantic review.

title: Select source spans by expression and archetype function
normative_requirement: A bounded A-roll selector shall choose eligible source spans using approved Expression Moments, phrase context, speaker, audio events, keyframes, target duration, archetype sequence, and must-preserve/must-exclude constraints.
trigger_preconditions: An admitted short-video job requires a concrete source sequence.
required_output_state: A candidate portfolio of source-span sequences with runtime, quote, beat, reason, and source context.
authority_invariants: No unsupported quote synthesis. | Selection retains enough context to preserve meaning. | Required reactions and expression tails remain eligible.
positive_acceptance: Given multiple takes or answer sections, when selection runs, then it ranks the sequence that best satisfies the approved archetype while preserving source truth.
adversarial_denial: Given a shorter clip that changes the answer's meaning, when compared, then it cannot win on pacing alone.
required_evidence: Source-context comparison, duration/coverage checks, candidate reasons, and independent selection review.

title: Enforce word-boundary EDL and audio continuity
normative_requirement: Accepted source cuts shall snap to validated word or permitted silence/audio-event boundaries, use declared padding, preserve speaker handoffs and reactions where required, and apply production-correct audio fades and output-time caption mapping.
trigger_preconditions: An A-roll candidate sequence is compiled into an EDL.
required_output_state: A mechanically validated EDL and audio/caption boundary plan.
authority_invariants: No mid-word cuts. | Timestamp drift is absorbed within declared padding. | Every cut works on both audio and video.
positive_acceptance: Given a cut at the end of a spoken phrase, when validated, then it lands on a word boundary, applies permitted padding, and does not create an audible pop.
adversarial_denial: Given a cut with a clean transcript boundary but a visible mouth discontinuity, when rendered evaluation runs, then it is not accepted solely by transcript validation.
required_evidence: Word-boundary tests, waveform/boundary checks, caption offset validation, and rendered cut inspection.

title: Compile evidence, B-roll, captions, and motion slots as source support
normative_requirement: The short-video Harness shall compile supporting visual treatments from source evidence, approved assets, keyframes, screenshots, diagrams, captions, and bounded motion-block requests, each with a declared narrative/Activative function, timing, geometry, and lineage.
trigger_preconditions: An accepted A-roll sequence identifies moments that require clarification, proof, emphasis, continuity, or visual relief.
required_output_state: Supporting-track plan and child asset/motion demands.
authority_invariants: Decorative novelty is insufficient justification. | Proof visuals require evidence lineage. | Face, captions, and source expression safe regions remain protected.
positive_acceptance: Given a numerical claim, when a data callout appears, then it is timed to the claim, cites the evidence object, and clears the subject/caption regions.
adversarial_denial: Given B-roll that contradicts the speaker or obscures a key reaction, when evaluation runs, then it fails.
required_evidence: Support-function validation, geometry checks, evidence references, and composition preview.

title: Evaluate rendered cut boundaries and whole-short coherence
normative_requirement: The evaluator shall inspect the actual rendered output at each cut, overlay transition, caption change, and selected checkpoints for visual jumps, audio pops, subtitle occlusion, wrong frames, grade consistency, source meaning, pacing, and archetype coherence.
trigger_preconditions: An encoded preview exists.
required_output_state: A boundary-level and whole-short evaluation report with time ranges, failure codes, evidence frames/waveforms, responsible layers, and verdict.
authority_invariants: Source inspection cannot substitute for rendered-output inspection. | Technical and semantic failures remain separate. | Producer self-description is not evidence.
positive_acceptance: Given a preview with a hidden subtitle at one overlay boundary, when evaluation runs, then the exact time and responsible layer are reported.
adversarial_denial: Given a technically perfect short that removes the source reaction and weakens the intended expression, when evaluated, then it fails the semantic/Activative gate.
required_evidence: Rendered timeline views, audio analysis, caption checks, VLM/human calibration, and verdict receipt.

title: Apply bounded short-video repair and operator revision
normative_requirement: Short-video failures or operator corrections shall compile into the smallest valid edit changes, preserve already-valid source selections and relationships, rerender only affected ranges or downstream outputs where possible, and stop after the configured repair limit or a human-owned boundary.
trigger_preconditions: A rendered evaluation or authorized operator request identifies a temporal defect or preference.
required_output_state: A ChangeRequestProgram, affected-node/range set, preview, new evaluation, and HumanResolutionEpisode where applicable.
authority_invariants: Repair cannot rewrite source speech. | Unaffected accepted nodes/checkpoints are reused. | Maximum repair rounds are enforced.
positive_acceptance: Given a caption collision at one scene, when repaired, then only the caption/scene render and dependent final export rerun.
adversarial_denial: Given three failed repair passes or an unresolved taste choice, when the limit is reached, then the system escalates rather than looping.
required_evidence: Change diff, invalidation map, reused checkpoint report, rerender hashes, evaluation, and human episode.

title: Produce source-grounded Carousel derivatives
normative_requirement: A Carousel job shall consume an approved archetype-coalition Final Script written through Guest Voice DNA, Primitive Coalition Contract, source spans, evidence, Expression Moments, and keyframes, and shall preserve source links for every slide while using Carousel-native swipe grammar.
trigger_preconditions: An approved source package and Carousel-capable Harness/profile are available.
required_output_state: Carousel Composition IR, slide-role plan, source/evidence map, rendered images, preview, and evaluation.
authority_invariants: Slides are not frame-time video scenes. | Editorial paraphrases are labeled. | Every source-backed assertion resolves to evidence.
positive_acceptance: Given an approved five-slide teaching route, when rendered, then each slide links to the supporting quote/evidence and the sequence preserves progression.
adversarial_denial: Given a quote slide whose text cannot be found in the source and is not labeled paraphrase, when validation runs, then it fails.
required_evidence: Slide/source traceability, geometry/text checks, sequence evaluation, and render hashes.

title: Produce source-grounded SuperVisual derivatives
normative_requirement: A SuperVisual job shall consume an approved archetype-coalition Final Script and Primitive Coalition Contract and shall create one source-grounded, feed-legible psychological role inside tension with exact source and transformation lineage.
trigger_preconditions: An approved source moment and SuperVisual-capable Harness/profile are available.
required_output_state: Single-frame Composition IR, source/evidence map, master image, variants, and evaluation.
authority_invariants: One frame cannot imply unsupported meaning. | Visual hierarchy is measurable. | Source quote and editorial language remain distinguishable.
positive_acceptance: Given an approved premise and portrait keyframe, when rendered, then the visual preserves identity prominence and cites its source.
adversarial_denial: Given a beautiful poster whose slogan overstates the speaker's claim, when evaluated, then it fails semantic fidelity.
required_evidence: Source map, feed-size legibility, reading-order evidence, semantic evaluation, and hashes.

title: Produce source-grounded 2D animation derivatives without activating Format 02
normative_requirement: Every approved archetype-coalition Final Script shall compile a source-grounded 2D Animation Scene Package for reusable B-roll, explainer, quote, concept, and Carousel use; the active archetype template and downstream compositions decide which scenes are fully rendered, without activating Format 02.
trigger_preconditions: An authorized non-Format-02 animation derivative job is admitted.
required_output_state: Animation scene/clip program, source/voice lineage, rig/layer/runtime bindings, rendered output, and evaluation.
authority_invariants: Format 02 remains deferred. | Generated performance does not claim to be the participant's actual reaction unless directly derived and labeled. | Identity references are governed.
positive_acceptance: Given a quote-driven animated explainer using the interviewer's audio, when produced, then every spoken segment and visual idea traces to the source or an explicitly approved derivative.
adversarial_denial: Given a Stretchy Studio rig demo, when no current animation Harness authorizes its use, then it cannot enter production.
required_evidence: Animation-job authority, source/voice map, runtime/rig receipts, identity/semantic evaluation, and Format 02 exclusion test.

title: Label quotations, condensations, voiceovers, and generated derivatives truthfully
normative_requirement: Verbatim quotations, Guest-Voice-DNA rewrites, editorial condensations, source-audio excerpts, generated voiceovers, and generated visual derivatives shall be explicitly distinguishable while preserving the approved Final Script and source lineage.
trigger_preconditions: A derivative transforms or reuses spoken language or voice.
required_output_state: Typed language/voice provenance records attached to content elements and exports.
authority_invariants: No paraphrase is presented as verbatim. | Synthetic or rerecorded voice is clearly identified in records. | Operator source authorization and identity use govern generation.
positive_acceptance: Given a Carousel condensation of a long answer, when displayed, then it is labeled editorial condensation and links to the source span.
adversarial_denial: Given a cloned voice reading newly written text without approved synthetic-voice operator source authorization, when production is attempted, then it is denied.
required_evidence: Quote-diff checks, voice/source metadata, operator source authorization validation, operator approval, and export disclosures.

title: Preserve cross-derivative identity, meaning, and batch continuity
normative_requirement: The batch shall evaluate whether shorts, Carousels, SuperVisuals, and animation derivatives remain coherent in identity, terminology, evidence, visual recurrence, and intended audience movement while allowing format-native variation.
trigger_preconditions: Two or more derivative jobs from the same source package reach candidate or accepted state.
required_output_state: A batch continuity report, shared/repeated element map, contradictions, fatigue/recurrence findings, and repair recommendations.
authority_invariants: Continuity does not require visual sameness. | Category-native grammar remains independent. | Contradictions are not hidden.
positive_acceptance: Given a short and Carousel using the same premise, when compared, then their core claim and source evidence agree while each uses its own format grammar.
adversarial_denial: Given a SuperVisual that reverses the short's call or misattributes a quote, when batch evaluation runs, then acceptance is blocked.
required_evidence: Cross-artifact semantic comparison, identity/source checks, recurrence analysis, and batch verdict.

title: Export complete derivative lineage and usage evidence
normative_requirement: Every derivative output shall package source-package and span references, ingredient versions, Harness and binding identities, transformation records, generated-asset lineage, evaluations, human resolutions, publication scope, and downstream usage acknowledgements.
trigger_preconditions: A derivative reaches terminal accepted, rejected, canceled, shadow, or published state.
required_output_state: A portable derivative evidence bundle and usage records.
authority_invariants: No accepted output is orphaned from source. | Restrictions and limitations travel with exports. | Historical versions remain addressable.
positive_acceptance: Given a published Carousel, when audited, then every slide's source/evidence and every generated visual's VAE lineage can be traced.
adversarial_denial: Given an exported animation with missing voice provenance, when package validation runs, then delivery fails.
required_evidence: Lineage completeness test, artifact hashes, usage acknowledgement, and portable bundle validation.

title: Compile natural-language operator revisions into typed change programs
normative_requirement: A Revision Compiler Programmed Model or eligible general fallback shall translate an authorized operator request, current state, target objects, Harness, JIT context, allowed tool registry, and evaluation into the smallest typed ChangeRequestProgram or an explicit clarification/escalation.
trigger_preconditions: An operator submits a revision in a category Studio or review queue.
required_output_state: A structured interpretation, exact operations, preconditions, invariants, affected nodes, validation plan, confidence, and escalation state.
authority_invariants: The compiler proposes commands; deterministic authority validation decides admission. | Unclear requests may ask one targeted question. | No unrestricted tool access.
positive_acceptance: Given 'let the reaction breathe half a second longer and lower the caption,' when context is sufficient, then the compiler emits exact temporal and layout operations with affected nodes.
adversarial_denial: Given 'make it better' with multiple plausible meanings, when confidence is insufficient, then it requests clarification rather than guessing.
required_evidence: Tool-call schema tests, human request benchmark, argument accuracy, over-edit rate, and interpretation receipt.

title: Convert direct manipulation into the same typed command path
normative_requirement: Dragging, trimming, splitting, resizing, cropping, reordering, selecting a candidate, or changing a parameter in the Studio shall produce the same canonical ChangeRequestProgram and validation path as an equivalent language request.
trigger_preconditions: An operator performs a direct manipulation and attempts to commit it.
required_output_state: A typed command/delta linked to before-state, target, user authority, and preview.
authority_invariants: The UI cannot mutate accepted state directly. | Undo and replay are command-based. | Equivalent actions have equivalent evidence.
positive_acceptance: Given a twelve-frame clip move, when committed, then the canonical program changes through a validated move operation and the same episode schema used for text revisions.
adversarial_denial: Given an unsupported canvas drag outside permitted geometry tolerance, when committed, then the command is rejected or converted into an amendment proposal.
required_evidence: UI-to-command round-trip tests, undo/replay, before/after hashes, and HumanResolutionEpisode.

title: Validate revision authority, scope, and minimality before execution
normative_requirement: Every ChangeRequestProgram shall pass schema, current-state identity, authority, tool grant, Transformation Contract, source truth, geometry/timing, budget, and minimal-change validation before preview or execution.
trigger_preconditions: A compiled or manually constructed change program is submitted.
required_output_state: An admitted dry-run/preview plan or typed denial with exact cause and next action.
authority_invariants: Valid existing relationships are preserved. | Out-of-scope meaning changes require the owning authority. | Stale-state commands cannot commit.
positive_acceptance: Given a caption shift within safe bounds, when validated, then it may preview without invalidating A-roll selection.
adversarial_denial: Given a stale command targeting a superseded composition version, when validation runs, then it is denied.
required_evidence: Schema/authority tests, stale-state race tests, minimal-diff analysis, and admission receipt.

title: Rerun only affected work and independently evaluate the result
normative_requirement: An accepted revision shall invalidate only directly changed objects and dependent nodes, reuse unaffected checkpoints and assets, render an appropriate preview, run required deterministic and independent evaluations, and either commit, reject, or escalate.
trigger_preconditions: Revision validation succeeds.
required_output_state: A selective rerun plan, new artifacts, evaluation, commit/reject state, and dependency updates.
authority_invariants: No full-batch rerun without dependency evidence. | Rejected preview leaves prior accepted state intact. | Repair limits prevent loops.
positive_acceptance: Given a SuperVisual text-size change, when executed, then only text layout, render, and dependent evaluation rerun.
adversarial_denial: Given a local edit that causes a new wrong-reading failure, when evaluated, then it is rejected and the prior accepted version remains current.
required_evidence: Checkpoint reuse report, invalidation graph, render diffs, evaluation, and state transaction receipt.

title: Emit a HumanResolutionEpisode for every meaningful human decision
normative_requirement: Every approval, rejection, candidate selection, revision, manual change, tool override, escalation resolution, taste explanation, and publication decision shall emit an immutable HumanResolutionEpisode linked to the exact before/after state, operations, evidence, authority, and applicability scope.
trigger_preconditions: An authorized human decision commits.
required_output_state: A complete HumanResolutionEpisode and automatic retrieval/programming-material disposition.
authority_invariants: No meaningful decision is stored only as UI telemetry. | Scope defaults to narrow. | Human rationale may remain optional but absence is explicit.
positive_acceptance: Given an operator rejects candidate A and selects B because A feels too polished, when committed, then the pair and reason are captured as scoped preference evidence.
adversarial_denial: Given an anonymous button click with no run or authority context, when persistence runs, then it cannot become a resolution episode.
required_evidence: Episode completeness validation, exact change/tool links, scope tests, and indexing receipt.

title: Use human-resolution evidence for governed retrieval and Programmed Model improvement
normative_requirement: Eligible HumanResolutionEpisodes shall become immediately retrievable precedents and may later produce supervised examples, preference pairs, hard negatives, repair trajectories, Steering Recipe evidence, or model-training candidates through offline operator-authorized dataset, shadow, evaluation, and promotion gates.
trigger_preconditions: A HumanResolutionEpisode closes and attribution completes.
required_output_state: Retrieval index entry, candidate learning dispositions, and later promotion artifacts where authorized.
authority_invariants: Immediate retrieval does not mean universal applicability. | Training eligibility follows operator-declared source authority. | No live weight mutation.
positive_acceptance: Given repeated accepted video corrections in one profile, when a future Revision Compiler is evaluated, then held-out episodes can test whether it predicts the same minimal operations.
adversarial_denial: Given one idiosyncratic workspace preference, when global recipe promotion is requested, then evidence scope blocks it.
required_evidence: Indexing tests, dataset eligibility, shadow replay, claim benchmark, and promotion receipt.

title: Launch campaigns from source packages or Asset Package Specs
normative_requirement: The Studio shall let an authorized operator select a workspace/project, inspect or complete a Canonical Interview Source Package, choose target outputs and Harnesses, set seed/taste direction, budget, deadline, autonomy mode, and launch a governed content batch.
trigger_preconditions: An operator wants to produce a batch from an interview source.
required_output_state: A Campaign Order, validated batch plan, bindings, budget reservation, and launch receipt.
authority_invariants: The Studio cannot invent missing Harnesses. | Source/source authority eligibility precedes launch. | Order data does not bypass product contracts.
positive_acceptance: Given an imported interview source, when the operator selects one short and one Carousel, then the Studio validates both Harnesses and launches one coordinated batch.
adversarial_denial: Given a requested Format 02 output before activation, when the order is validated, then that job is blocked and the rest may proceed if eligible.
required_evidence: Order schema, source/Harness validation, budget checks, autonomy binding, and launch receipt.

title: Operate one shared Studio shell with category-native modules
normative_requirement: The product shall provide one shared workspace, project, campaign, run, review, evidence, artifact, worker, provider, budget, and publishing shell while registering Interview Expression, Video Production, Static Composition, Visual Asset, and Knowledge/Programmed Model modules behind current product boundaries.
trigger_preconditions: An operator navigates across source, production, review, or learning work.
required_output_state: Shared shell routes and category-module read/action contracts.
authority_invariants: Modules do not duplicate canonical state or authority. | A module loads only relevant tools and evidence. | Future Character Performance remains deferred.
positive_acceptance: Given a source-led batch, when the operator moves from Interview to Video to Static review, then all modules display the same source and campaign identities.
adversarial_denial: Given a module attempting to own VAE state locally, when architecture tests run, then the duplication is rejected.
required_evidence: Route/read-model integration tests, shared identity consistency, product-boundary tests, and accessibility review.

title: Support AUTOPILOT, REVIEW_BEFORE_SHIP, CHECKPOINTED, and SHADOW modes
normative_requirement: Campaign and Harness bindings shall declare one autonomy mode, allowed human gates, publication authority, exception policy, and mode changes, with mature Harnesses targeting AUTOPILOT or REVIEW_BEFORE_SHIP rather than continuous manual editing.
trigger_preconditions: A campaign is configured or its governance mode changes.
required_output_state: A versioned autonomy policy and mode-specific workflow behavior.
authority_invariants: Mode changes are authorized and receipted. | SHADOW never publishes. | AUTOPILOT still escalates genuine authority or operator-owned creative conflicts.
positive_acceptance: Given CHECKPOINTED mode, when a declared creative gate is reached, then the Studio pauses with a complete decision package.
adversarial_denial: Given AUTOPILOT mode and a deterministic validation pass, when no human-owned conflict exists, then the system does not ask for approval.
required_evidence: Mode transition tests, gate/publish enforcement, exception simulations, and receipts.

title: Provide exception-only review packages and attributable decisions
normative_requirement: When human attention is required, the Studio shall present the exact issue, source and current state, candidates or alternatives, evaluator evidence, authority owner, costs and downstream effects, and permitted actions without forcing the operator to inspect raw logs or rebuild the work.
trigger_preconditions: A human gate, taste boundary, policy conflict, budget change, or unrecoverable exception occurs.
required_output_state: A structured ReviewPackage and subsequent HumanResolutionEpisode.
authority_invariants: Only assigned authority sees actionable decisions. | Deterministic facts are not disguised as creative choices. | Unavailable evidence is disclosed.
positive_acceptance: Given two valid composition candidates with unresolved taste, when review opens, then the operator sees their source lineage, differences, evaluations, and expected downstream effects.
adversarial_denial: Given a worker crash with an automatic retry policy, when no human action is needed, then it remains an operational incident rather than a creative review.
